summary: time-format-kit postinstall exfiltrates SSH/AWS credentials and performs SSRF against internal host
details: time-format-kit is a typosquat presenting itself as a dayjs helper. The package's postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and constructs child_process.exec via runtime string concatenation (require('ch'+'il'+'d_p'+'rocess')['e'+'xec']) to invoke three base64-decoded shell payloads. The first reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/authorized_keys, ~/.ssh/known_hosts, ~/.bash_history, environment variables, and the output of hostname/whoami/id/sudo -l, base64-encodes the collected data, and posts it via curl to a hardcoded attacker OAST subdomain at oastify.com. A second payload fetches http://tst.woa.com/flag.html from the installer's network position and forwards the response to the same attacker collaborator. A third payload invokes http://tst.woa.com/ssrf_forward.php with the attacker's OAST host as the forward target. The 'Dayjs core polyfill' comment and package description are cover text.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
